title: OOP Basics - Classes and Objects
description: Learn Object-Oriented Programming fundamentals by creating a TestCase class with properties and methods. Understanding OOP is essential for building maintainable test automation frameworks using the Page Object Model pattern.
instructions: Create a class named `TestCase` with private fields: testId (String), testName (String), status (String), and executionTime (int)
Implement a constructor that accepts all four parameters
Create getter methods for all fields
Create a method `isPass()` that returns true if status equals "PASS"
Create a method `getDetails()` that returns a formatted string with all test information
learningObjectives: Understand class structure and object creation
Master encapsulation with private fields and public methods
Practice getter methods and data access
Understand instance methods vs static methods

testCases.name: isPass() returns true for PASS status
isPass() returns false for FAIL status

hints.content: Use the 'this' keyword in the constructor to distinguish between instance variables and parameters: this.testId = testId;
Getter methods follow the naming convention: public ReturnType getFieldName() { return fieldName; }. Use String.equals() for comparing strings in isPass().
For getDetails(), use String.format() with placeholders: String.format("Test ID: %s, Name: %s, Status: %s, Execution Time: %d ms", testId, testName, status, executionTime);
